## PERFIL

**Oren Klaff** e um investment banker, autor e especialista em pitch que transformou neurociencia aplicada em metodologia de persuasao e fechamento de deals. Formado em neurofinance, Klaff levantou mais de $400 milhoes em capital ao longo de sua carreira na Intersection Capital. Autor dos bestsellers "Pitch Anything" (2011) e "Flip the Script" (2019), ele e reconhecido como uma das maiores autoridades mundiais em frame control, social dynamics e a ciencia por tras do processo de tomada de decisao humana.

Seu diferencial em relacao a outros especialistas em vendas e a fundacao neurocientifica: Klaff nao ensina "tecnicas de vendas" â€” ele ensina como o cerebro reptiliano (crocodile brain) filtra informacoes e como estruturar comunicacao que sobrevive a esse filtro. A abordagem e radicalmente diferente do "rapport-based selling" tradicional.

---

## CONTRIBUICAO CENTRAL

A contribuicao de Klaff ao sistema pode ser resumida em quatro eixos:

### 1. Frame Control como Fundacao de Toda Interacao Social
Para Klaff, frames â€” e nao argumentos â€” governam todas as interacoes sociais. Quem controla o frame, controla a conversacao. O frame mais forte sempre absorve o mais fraco. Esta filosofia permeia toda sua metodologia e e o alicerce conceitual do S.T.R.O.N.G. Method. ^[FIL-OK-001, FIL-OK-002, FIL-OK-003]

### 2. Neuroscience-Based Pitching (Crocodile Brain Theory)
Klaff identificou que toda mensagem passa primeiro pelo crocodile brain (tronco cerebral) antes de chegar ao neocortex. Se a mensagem nao sobrevive ao filtro do croc brain â€” que processa ameaca, novidade e contexto social â€” ela nunca sera avaliada racionalmente. Isso inverte a logica trad

## PADROES DE PENSAMENTO

### Neurological-First Reasoning

**Descricao:**
Klaff consistently reasons from neuroscience outward. He doesn't start with "what should I say" â€” he starts with "how does the brain process what I say." Every tactical recommendation traces back to a neurological mechanism: croc brain filtering, dopamine response, hot vs cold cognition, SCARF activation. This is not metaphor for him â€” it's the literal operating system of persuasion.

**Exemplos de uso:**
- When designing a pitch structure, starts from croc brain filtering (novelty, threat, social relevance) rather than from content logic
- When diagnosing a failed pitch, looks for neurological failures (cold cognition dominance, status crash, croc brain rejection) before examining content quality

### Frame-Collision Worldview

**Descricao:**
Klaff sees every human interaction as a frame collision. There is no neutral ground â€” every meeting, every call, every email is a frame battle. One frame wins, one dies. This creates a binary thinking style: "Who owns the frame right now?" is the first question he asks about any situation. Not "what was said" or "what was the content" but "whose frame dominated."

## OVERVIEW

The StoryBrand Marketing RoadMap is a practical implementation course that teaches businesses to create 6 core marketing tools using the StoryBrand framework. While "Sell With Story" (SWS) focused on the BrandScript foundation and storytelling theory, SBMR is the EXECUTION course â€” how to actually build websites, write emails, create sales letters, generate leads, and gather testimonials.

**Co-Teacher: Ray Edwards** â€” Author of "Copy That Sells", once paid $180,000 for a single sales letter. Creator of the PASTOR Framework. Teaches the 7 Persuasion Principles.

**Guest Expert: Francis Jones** â€” Infusionsoft (now Keap) specialist. Covers email marketing automation, nurture sequences, and the Legacy Traditional charter school case study.

---

## MODULE STRUCTURE

| Module | Topic | Files | Key Content |
|--------|-------|-------|-------------|
| 01 | Create & Understand BrandScript | 4 | BrandScript deep-dive, Jeb Bush case study, political messaging |
| 02 | Wireframe a Website | 5 | 10-section website architecture, FITZEE case, LibertyID review |
| 03 | Email Nurture Campaigns | 5 | 7 Deadly Sins, 7 Things Every Email Needs, Francis Jones interview, live campaign walkthrough |
| 04 | Write a Sales Letter | 4 | PASTOR Framework, 7 Persuasion Principles, 15 Building Blocks, pet store example |
| 05 | Lead-Generating PDFs | 2 | 5 types of PDFs, clickbait titles, reciprocity engine |
| 06 | Testimonials + Putting It All Together | 2 | Testimonia

## FONTE 2: STORYBRAND MARKETING REPORT (SBMR)

> **Elementos:** 52 | **Arquivos:** ~15 | **Co-teachers:** Ray Edwards, Francis Jones

### PASTOR Framework (Ray Edwards)

O PASTOR Framework Ã© a metodologia central de sales copy ensinada no SBMR, criada por Ray Edwards e integrada ao sistema StoryBrand:

| Letra | Significado | AplicaÃ§Ã£o |
|-------|-------------|-----------|
| **P** | Person/Problem/Pain | Identificar a pessoa, seu problema e dor especÃ\xadfica |
| **A** | Amplify | Amplificar as consequÃªncias de NÃƒO resolver |
| **S** | Story/Solution | Contar histÃ³ria + apresentar soluÃ§Ã£o |
| **T** | Testimony/Transformation | Prova social + transformaÃ§Ã£o demonstrada |
| **O** | Offer | Apresentar a oferta com clareza |
| **R** | Response | CTA claro â€” dizer exatamente o que fazer |

### 7 Persuasion Principles

1. **Reciprocity** â€” DÃª valor primeiro (lead generator, PDFs gratuitos)
2. **Social Proof** â€” Testemunhos, cases, nÃºmeros
3. **Authority** â€” Posicione-se como guia competente
4. **Liking** â€” Empatia genuÃ\xadna pelo problema do cliente
5. **Scarcity** â€” Limite real (tempo, vagas, bÃ´nus)
6. **Commitment/Consistency** â€” Pequenos "sins" levam ao grande "sim"
7. **Unity** â€” Pertencimento a um grupo/movimento

### 15 Building Blocks of Sales Copy

Estrutura modular para construir qualquer peÃ§a de copy:
1. Pre-head / Eyebrow copy

3. Sub-headline
4. Opening (hero's problem)
5. Pain amplification
6. Story/Solution bridge
7. Benefits (not features)
8. Testimonials/Social proof
9. Offer presentation
10. Value stack

12. Guarantee/Risk reversal
13. Urgency/Scarcity
14. CTA (primary + secondary)
15. P.S. / Reinforce

## TOP INSIGHTS

### Insight 1: The 89% Rule
- 89% positive reviews are MORE persuasive than 90% or 100%
- 4.2-4.7 star rating is the sweet spot (not 4.8+)
- Imperfection signals authenticity

### Insight 2: Gift vs Reward Asymmetry
- $5 gift upfront = 52% compliance
- $50 reward promised = 23% compliance
- Gift approach is 8x more cost-effective

### Insight 3: Weakness-First Strategy
- Mentioning a weakness EARLY then bridging with "but" to strengths
- Avis: 700% market share increase
- L'Oreal: 300% market share increase
- Charity recession: 36% more donations by just resequencing

### Insight 4: The Scarcity Double Whammy
- Scarcity alone: 2.4x sales (10â†’24 carloads)
- Scarcity + exclusive information: 6x sales (10â†’60 carloads)
- "The information that it was scarce was itself scarce"

### Insight 5: Public Goals Power
- Private goals: 14% success rate
- Public goals: 86% success rate
- The difference is accountability + consistency pressure

### Insight 6: Active Commitment ("Will You?")
- "Please call if you need to cancel" â†’ 30% no-shows
- "Will you please call?" + pause â†’ 10% no-shows
- Two words + one pause = 67% reduction

### Insight 7: Pre-suasion Priming
- Fluffy clouds on landing page â†’ comfort-oriented purchases
- Pennies on landing page â†’ price-oriented purchases
- What people see FIRST determines what they value

### Insight 8: Social Proof Localization
- Generic social proof: 72% compliance
- Region-specific: 79% compliance
- Town-specific: 83% compliance
- The "5 Billion Pound Let

## L1 PHILOSOPHIES (FIL-AH)

```yaml
L1_PHILOSOPHIES:
  - id: "FIL-AH-403"
    content: "Lead generation is the lifeblood of the business. If you don't have the first of the five core functions in, the business won't succeed."
    source: "03-Hybrid/02-Lead Gen/02-2. Making Your Ads/transcription_mp4_03-Advanced_Lead_Gen_Principles.txt"
    domains: [lead-generation, business-fundamentals]

  - id: "FIL-AH-404"
    content: "Revenue is directly proportional to how much you spend on marketing. If something creates a 4:1, 5:1, or 10:1 return, you should spend more on that thing."
    source: "03-Hybrid/02-Lead Gen/02-2. Making Your Ads/transcription_mp4_03-Advanced_Lead_Gen_Principles.txt"
    domains: [ad-spend, marketing-investment]

  - id: "FIL-AH-405"
    content: "Failure and invention are inseparable twins. You must be willing to pay a certain amount for failure. Three quarters of ads won't be winners, but once you have winners, you can scale those. That's the game."
    source: "03-Hybrid/02-Lead Gen/02-2. Making Your Ads/transcription_mp4_03-Advanced_Lead_Gen_Principles.txt"
    domains: [testing-mindset, advertising]

  - id: "FIL-AH-406"
    content: "There will always be eyeballs. People always want to lose weight. The average woman diets five times a year. As long as you have a profitable acquisition process, you can afford to buy traffic on any platform."
    source: "03-Hybrid/02-Lead Gen/02-2. Making Your Ads/transcription_mp4_03-Advanced_Lead_Gen_Principles.txt"
    domains: [market-demand, traffic-acquisition]

  - id: "FIL-AH-407"
    content: "You are not your customer. Don't project what YOU would opt into. It's about ROI and what converts, not what looks cool or aligns with your brand."
    source: "03-Hybrid/02-Lead Gen/02-2. Making Your Ads/transcription_mp4_01-Why_You_Should_Make_Your_Own_Ads.txt"
    domains: [advertising-psychology, customer-empathy]

  - id: "FIL-AH-408"
    content: "No one knows you exist. Nine out of ten people who respond to Orange Theory ads have never heard of Orange Theory. Don't worry about branding â€” run profitable converting campaigns because that IS how you build your brand."
    source: "03-Hybrid/02-Lead Gen/02-2. Making Your Ads/transcription_mp4_01-Why_You_Should_Make_Your_Own_Ads.txt"
    domains: [brand-building, advertising]

  - id: "FIL-AH-409"
    content: "The Ham and the Garlic: Give people what they want (the ham) so you can give them what they need (the garlic) later. Meet people where they are, not where you want them to be."
    source: "03-Hybrid/02-Lead Gen/02-2. Making Your Ads/transcription_mp4_01-Why_You_Should_Make_Your_Own_Ads.txt"
    domains: [marketing-psychology, customer-journey]

  - id: "FIL-AH-410"
    content: "He who can spend the most to acquire a customer will win. The people who can provide the most value to end users will win."
    source: "03-Hybrid/02-Lead Gen/08-8. Hybrid Phase 3/transcription_mp4_02-Watch_If_You_Need_Conviction.txt"
    domains: [customer-acquisition, competitive-advantage]

  - id: "FIL-AH-411"
    content: "Learning to make it rain (generate demand) is the most valuable skill above sales. Sales lets you make $500/hour; lead gen lets you buy back all your time."
    source: "01-Gym Launch 2.0/02-Lead Gen/01-1. Lead Gen Overview/transcription_mp4_02-Lead_Generation.txt"
    domains: [skill-hierarchy, entrepreneurship]

  - id: "FIL-AH-412"
    content: "Every single day, thousands of people sign up for a gym. Every single one of them responded to marketing. No one walks in and signs up without responding to marketing of some sort."
    source: "03-Hybrid/02-Lead Gen/02-2. Making Your Ads/transcription_mp4_03-Advanced_Lead_Gen_Principles.txt"
    domains: [marketing-fundamentals, demand-capture]

  - id: "FIL-AH-413"
    content: "You can't be both busy and broke. You can always trade time for money. If you have time, you can trade it for money. They go back and forth."
    source: "01-Gym Launch 2.0/02-Lead Gen/06-6. Setting Up Your Sales Calendar/transcription_mp4_03-Sales_Availability_Fundamentals.txt"
    domains: [time-money-tradeoff, entrepreneurship]

  - id: "FIL-AH-414"
    content: "Workouts are only 10% of the equation. We know people who've been at your gym for a year still look the same. The things that drive change are coaching and food, not the gym equipment."
    source: "03-Hybrid/02-Lead Gen/08-8. Hybrid Phase 3/transcription_mp4_02-Watch_If_You_Need_Conviction.txt"
    domains: [fitness-business, value-proposition]

  - id: "FIL-AH-415"
    content: "People need to invest. People pay, they pay attention. When people pay $5,000 for a weight loss program, they lose more weight than people paying $5. More skin in the game = better results."
    source: "01-Gym Launch 2.0/02-Lead Gen/01-1. Expectations/transcription_mp4_01-Expectations.txt"
    domains: [pricing-psychology, commitment]

  - id: "FIL-AH-416"
    content: "Don't be cute. Don't have an ego. The gyms who do best have the discipline to stay boring and consistent and repeat processes that are successful. There are no silver bullets."
    source: "01-Gym Launch 2.0/02-Lead Gen/01-1. Expectations/transcription_mp4_01-Expectations.txt"
    domains: [discipline, process-adherence]

  - id: "FIL-AH-417"
    content: "Speed is king. The fastest you do things, the better you do. The fastest you roll out new materials, ads â€” speed beats perfection every time."
    source: "01-Gym Launch 2.0/02-Lead Gen/01-1. Expectations/transcription_mp4_01-Expectations.txt"
    domains: [execution-speed, entrepreneurship]

  - id: "FIL-AH-418"
    content: "Do the boring work that makes you money. Business is about better, not new. Better isn't sexy, but better is what makes business better."
    source: "01-Gym Launch 2.0/02-Lead Gen/01-1. Expectations/transcription_mp4_01-Expectations.txt"
    domains: [work-ethic, incremental-improvement]
```

## L2 MENTAL MODELS (MM-AH)

```yaml
L2_MENTAL_MODELS:
  - id: "MM-AH-403"
    content: "Lead Gen Scrambler (Content Twister): A matrix of all ad variables â€” headlines, copy lengths, page names, image types, video types, enhancements â€” that you mix and match to create infinite ad variations. Gives endless combinations without changing the core offer."
    source: "03-Hybrid/02-Lead Gen/02-2. Making Your Ads/transcription_mp4_02-Lead_Gen_Scrambler.txt + PDF"
    domains: [advertising, creative-testing, lead-generation]

  - id: "MM-AH-404"
    content: "Three Levers Model: 2x Customers vs 2x Retention vs 2x Price. Price has disproportionate impact on profit â€” doubling price on a 15% margin business doubles profit even with a tiny increase. Price > Retention > Customer count for profit impact."
    source: "01-Gym Launch 2.0/02-Lead Gen/02-2. Launch Overview/transcription_mp4_02-83k_Per_Month_Blueprint.txt"
    domains: [pricing, business-model, profit-optimization]

  - id: "MM-AH-405"
    content: "Relative vs Absolute ROI: Better to get 3:1 ROI spending $20K (making $60K) than 20:1 ROI spending $500 (making $10K). Absolute dollar profit matters more than percentage ROI."
    source: "01-Gym Launch 2.0/02-Lead Gen/01-1. Expectations/transcription_mp4_01-Expectations.txt"
    domains: [ad-spend-scaling, roi-analysis]

  - id: "MM-AH-406"
    content: "Banner Blindness Breaker: People stop seeing your ads when they look the same. The solution is changing creative (not offers). Different pages, different looks, different angles, different backgrounds â€” same offer, different wrapping paper."
    source: "03-Hybrid/02-Lead Gen/02-2. Making Your Ads/transcription_mp4_03-Advanced_Lead_Gen_Principles.txt"
    domains: [advertising, creative-strategy]

  - id: "MM-AH-407"
    content: "Funnel Algebra (EPC Model): Revenue = Impressions Ã— CTR Ã— Opt-in Rate Ã— Close Rate Ã— Avg Ticket. ROAS â‰  ROI â€” must account for fulfillment costs. Real EPC and real max CPL are the metrics that matter, vanity metrics like gross ROAS deceive."
    source: "04-In-GYM Launch/02-Lead Gen/06-6. Advanced Funnel Strategies/transcription_mp4_02-Advanced_Funnel_Math.txt"
    domains: [funnel-math, unit-economics, advertising]

  - id: "MM-AH-408"
    content: "Promotion Formula: Duration + Benefit + Power Word = Headline. Examples: '6-Week Fit Mom Challenge', '42-Day Metabolic Reset', '28-Day Transformation Jump Start'. Same offer, different wrapping paper."
    source: "03-Hybrid/02-Lead Gen/02-2. Making Your Ads/transcription_mp4_04-Ad_Flow_Offer_Overview.txt"
    domains: [copywriting, offer-creation, headlines]

  - id: "MM-AH-409"
    content: "Three Funnel Types for Service: 1) Opt-in funnel (volume, low-to-mid ticket, start here), 2) Application funnel (quality, high ticket), 3) Widget + Application (free lead magnet â†’ application on thank you page). Sequence: Volume first â†’ Quality second."
    source: "03-Hybrid/02-Lead Gen/10-10. Advanced Funnel Strategies/transcription_mp4_01-Advanced_Funnel_Strategy_Overview.txt"
    domains: [funnel-strategy, lead-qualification]

  - id: "MM-AH-410"
    content: "Synchrony Effect: Groups of people doing the same exercise at the same time in unison draws people's attention at a subconscious/amygdala level. It signals safety, community, and belonging. 75-80% of top-performing ad videos are this style."
    source: "03-Hybrid/02-Lead Gen/09-9. Advanced Lead Gen/transcription_mp4_01-Optin_Videos___Group_Workout_Strategy.txt"
    domains: [advertising-psychology, video-strategy]

  - id: "MM-AH-411"
    content: "Retargeting Strategy: Run content/value video ad for impressions â†’ create custom audience of people who watched 75%+ â†’ retarget that audience with direct response ad at $5/day. Retargeted audiences are small but high-quality and cheap to reach."
    source: "03-Hybrid/02-Lead Gen/09-9. Advanced Lead Gen/transcription_mp4_07-Retargeting_Strategies.txt"
    domains: [retargeting, advertising-strategy]

  - id: "MM-AH-412"
    content: "Two Traffic Sources Model: Paid (money for leads) + Organic (time for leads). Both have costs â€” paid costs money, organic costs time. Answer is always BOTH because they compound and cut time to goal in half."
    source: "01-Gym Launch 2.0/02-Lead Gen/02-2. Launch Overview/transcription_mp4_02-83k_Per_Month_Blueprint.txt"
    domains: [traffic-strategy, lead-generation]

  - id: "MM-AH-413"
    content: "Messenger Setter Model: Three-step organic sales process â€” 1) Message outbound, 2) Get 10-min warm-up call, 3) Set 60-min close call. The messaging is a DATE not a sale â€” Open â†’ Relate â†’ Transition to fitness â†’ Clarify goals â†’ Label problem â†’ Overview past pains â†’ Create curiosity â†’ Phone call."
    source: "01-Gym Launch 2.0/02-Lead Gen/09-9. Organic Lead Gen/transcription_mp4_04-Messenger_Setter_Script.txt"
    domains: [organic-lead-gen, sales-process, messaging]

  - id: "MM-AH-414"
    content: "Price Anchoring Model: Include one-on-one personal training at $36K/year at the top of your pricing sheet. This psychologically breaks the barrier, making $3-4K programs seem like a steal by comparison."
    source: "01-Gym Launch 2.0/02-Lead Gen/02-2. Launch Overview/transcription_mp4_02-83k_Per_Month_Blueprint.txt"
    domains: [pricing-psychology, sales-strategy]

  - id: "MM-AH-415"
    content: "Copy Power Words Hierarchy: FREE and NEW are the two most powerful words in marketing. 'Because' is the most powerful persuasion word â€” people comply when you give any reason at all."
    source: "03-Hybrid/02-Lead Gen/02-2. Making Your Ads/transcription_mp4_02-Lead_Gen_Scrambler.txt"
    domains: [copywriting, persuasion]
```

## L3 HEURISTICS (HEUR-AH)

```yaml
L3_HEURISTICS:
  - id: "HEUR-AH-403"
    content: "Cost Per Lead target: Under $25/lead = doing great. $30-40 = fine (still 4-5:1 ROI). Average $8-13 is good range for Facebook. $15+ consider pausing the ad. Don't arbitrarily cap at $10."
    source: "01-Gym Launch 2.0/02-Lead Gen/01-1. Expectations/transcription_mp4_01-Expectations.txt + 04-4. How to Optimize/transcription_mp4_03-Ad_Set_Budget_Optimization.txt"
    domains: [ad-optimization, benchmarks]
    type: numerical

  - id: "HEUR-AH-404"
    content: "Average first-month ROI: 5.5:1. Average upfront cash per sale: $850. If you can get $850 upfront at 20% close rate from $25 leads, you break even at 5 sales for every $125 spent."
    source: "01-Gym Launch 2.0/02-Lead Gen/01-1. Expectations/transcription_mp4_01-Expectations.txt"
    domains: [roi-benchmarks, unit-economics]
    type: numerical

  - id: "HEUR-AH-405"
    content: "Recommended daily ad spend: $100/day minimum across 6-10 ad sets. Start each ad set at $5-10/day. Less than $100/day takes too long to find winners."
    source: "01-Gym Launch 2.0/02-Lead Gen/01-1. Expectations/transcription_mp4_01-Expectations.txt + 06-6. Optimizing & Scaling Ads.txt"
    domains: [ad-budget, scaling]
    type: numerical

  - id: "HEUR-AH-406"
    content: "72-Hour Rule: Never judge an ad before 72 hours. Winners often look like losers in the first 2 days. Day 3-5 turnarounds are common. Facebook needs ~8,000 impressions to learn a new ad (3-7 days)."
    source: "03-Hybrid/02-Lead Gen/02-2. Making Your Ads/transcription_mp4_03-Advanced_Lead_Gen_Principles.txt + 06-6. Optimizing & Scaling.txt"
    domains: [ad-optimization, testing]
    type: numerical

  - id: "HEUR-AH-407"
    content: "Ad Testing Volume: Test 300 ads when spending $50K. Make 5 new ads every week minimum. Only put ads in portal if they convert in 80%+ of test markets. There's no secret â€” just volume."
    source: "03-Hybrid/02-Lead Gen/02-2. Making Your Ads/transcription_mp4_02-Lead_Gen_Scrambler.txt"
    domains: [creative-testing, ad-volume]

L4_FRAMEWORKS:

    content: |
      Lead Gen Scrambler Matrix: 5 variable categories to mix-match for infinite ad variations:
      1. HEADLINE: Duration + Challenge type + Gender + Benefit/Status + Curiosity + Fear + Negative
      2. COPY: Short/Medium/Long + Because/Reason-why + Benefits-tied-to-status + So-that-you-can + Poetic/Visual + Implied authority + Social proof + Scarcity/Urgency/Deadline
      3. PAGE NAME: Business page + Personal name + "Best [local area] fitness" + "Best [local area] deals"
      4. IMAGES: Enhanced colors + Banners + Diagonal lines + Groups + Testimonials + Black-and-white + Workout pics + Selfies + Eyes-into-camera + Meme treatment
      5. VIDEOS: Pitch + Facility tour + Whiteboard + Group testimonial + Average Joe testimonial + Carousel testimonial + Synchronized group workout + Boomerang/GIF + Professional vs iPhone + Different backgrounds/scenery

    content: |
      Messenger Setter Script (8-Step Organic Sales):
      1. OPEN: "Hey [Name], what's going on in your world today?"
      2. RELATE (Warm): "I saw you commented/liked my post, we haven't connected"
      2. RELATE (Cold): Find relatable element from profile (kids, dogs, hobbies)
      3. TRANSITION: "Does [their situation] make it hard to eat healthy/work out?"
      4. CLARIFY GOALS: "What are you trying to do fitness-wise right now?"
      5. LABEL WITH PROBLEM: "So you're trying to lose a few lbs, totally get it"
      6. OVERVIEW PAST PAIN: "What have you tried before? How'd that work?"
      7. CREATE CURIOSITY: "I have suggestions for you (if you even want to hear them)"
      8. PHONE CALL: "It would take 23,498 pages to type out, can we hop on a quick call?"
      Golden Rule: Every response = Acknowledge + Encourage + Next Question. Statement then question, always.
    source: "01-Gym Launch 2.0/02-Lead Gen/09-9. Organic Lead Gen/transcription_mp4_04-Messenger_Setter_Script.txt + PDF"
    domains: [organic-lead-gen, messaging, sales-scripts]

  - id: "FW-AH-408"
    content: |

      - Profile pic: Face only, smiling, professional/portrait mode
      - About section: "I help [WHO] do [WHAT] without [BOO]"

  - id: "FW-AH-409"
    content: |
      Retargeting Architecture:
      Option A: Content video as conversion ad (soft pitch with CTA) â€” simpler, works in local markets with trust
      Option B: Content video for impressions/video views â†’ Custom audience of 75%+ viewers â†’ Direct response ad targeting only that audience at $5/day
      Both work. Test both. Content-first retargeting produces higher quality but lower volume applications.
    source: "03-Hybrid/02-Lead Gen/09-9. Advanced Lead Gen/transcription_mp4_07-Retargeting_Strategies.txt"

  - id: "FW-AH-410"
    content: |
      Hybrid Bolt-On (Internal Upsell) Framework:
      Prerequisite: 50+ clients or ~$15K/month from cold traffic
      1. Create hook/event (birthday, seasonal, new program)
      2. Announce: "I'm meeting with EVERY client one-on-one" (don't say "let me know if interested")
      3. Schedule ALL existing clients for 1-on-1 meeting
      4. Sell hybrid coaching: $49-100/week depending on current vs new
      5. Use their track record: If struggling â†’ "You need more accountability". If successful â†’ "Imagine with coaching on top"
      Result: 50% close rate Ã— 100 meetings Ã— $200/month = $120K/year additional revenue
    source: "03-Hybrid/02-Lead Gen/07-7. Hybrid Phase 2/transcription_mp4_01-Hybrid_Bolt_On_Overview.txt"
    domains: [upselling, hybrid-model, revenue-expansion]

  - id: "FW-AH-411"
    content: |
      Copywriting Status Elevation: Level 1 = Benefits (meal plan, workouts). Level 2 = Benefits tied to STATUS.
      Template: "[Feature] so that you can [status-elevating benefit]"
      Examples: "Meal plan so good everyone will be jealous" / "So that you'll be the fittest mom in the neighborhood" / "So you never have to think about what to cook ever again"
      Make it poetic (describe feeling), make it visual (steam rising off your abs), imply authority, add social proof.
    source: "03-Hybrid/02-Lead Gen/02-2. Making Your Ads/transcription_mp4_03-Advanced_Lead_Gen_Principles.txt + transcription_mp4_02-Lead_Gen_Scrambler.txt"
    domains: [copywriting, persuasion, advertising]

  - id: "FW-AH-412"
    content: |
      Lead Nurture Sequence (Organic Cold-to-Close):
      After 10-min warm-up call:
      1. Send personalized email template OR application link (Google Form/Wufoo)
      2. Email includes: recap + rapport + series of qualifying questions + commitment questions ("willing to invest financially?" / "scale of 1-10")
      3. If commitment < 10: Follow up asking why not
      4. If spouse needed: Invite to same call time
      5. Reminder sequence for show-up
      Application link is more scalable; personalized email has higher response rate.
    source: "01-Gym Launch 2.0/02-Lead Gen/09-9. Organic Lead Gen/transcription_mp4_08-Cold_Messenger_Lead_Nurture.txt"
    domains: [lead-nurture, sales-process]

  - id: "FW-AH-413"
    content: |
      Facebook Compliance Rules (How to Not Get Banned):
      - Landing page must deliver what's promised in ad copy
      - Landing page must offer ONE thing only
      - Must have trusted elements: terms, privacy policy, address, website on ALL pages
      - Cannot promise specific results ("lose 6 pounds in 6 days")
      - Cannot call out users or make them feel insecure ("are you fat?")
      - Cannot promote ideal body image
      - No copyrighted music in videos
      - Avoid logging in from multiple locations rapidly
      - Never say specific weight loss + time period in testimonials ("lost 20lbs in 6 weeks")
      - Acceptable: "Since I've been with [trainer], I've lost three dress sizes"
    source: "01-Gym Launch 2.0/02-Lead Gen/03-3. Creating Your Ads/transcription_mp4_09-Warning-_How_To_Not_Get_Banned.txt"
    domains: [advertising-compliance, facebook-policies]

    content: |
      Video Ad Recording Process (6-8 new videos per day):
      Step 1: Place iPhone/iPad on gym floor
      Step 2: Trainers record finishers or group exercises with everyone in unison
      Step 3: Daily rotation of camera angles (middle, corner, elevated, side, walking in, outside)
      Step 4: Count down cadence for synchrony (1-2-3, 1-2-3)
      Step 5: Select best daily clips
      Step 6: Edit with InShot: meme treatment, banners, subtitles, emojis, contrast
      Step 7: Give $50 bonus to trainer whose video performs best that week
      Result: 40 raw videos/week â†’ 5-6 ad-ready videos after selection
    source: "03-Hybrid/02-Lead Gen/09-9. Advanced Lead Gen/transcription_mp4_01-Optin_Videos___Group_Workout_Strategy.txt"
    domains: [content-creation, video-strategy, gym-operations]

  - id: "MET-AH-405"
    content: |
      Cold Messenger Outreach Process (Daily):
      Step 1: Exhaust warm leads first (people who liked/commented/followed your content)
      Step 2: Search hashtags on Instagram (Recent, not Top) â€” #fitmom, #fitjourney, #momlife
      Step 3: Look for normal people who need help (skip coaches, skip already-fit people)
      Step 4: Message with opener + relate (find common ground from profile)
      Step 5: Transition to fitness within 3-4 messages
      Step 6: Follow Setter Script through 8 steps
      Step 7: Get on phone THAT DAY â€” "If they have time to message you, they have 5 minutes for a call"
      Step 8: TKAD â€” Time Kills All Deals. Complete entire process in 48 hours or less.
      Volume: 25-50 outreach/day â†’ 5 calls â†’ 1-2 sales/day at top performance
    source: "01-Gym Launch 2.0/02-Lead Gen/09-9. Organic Lead Gen/transcription_mp4_04-Messenger_Setter_Script.txt + 07-Two_Types_of_Organic.txt"
    domains: [organic-lead-gen, outreach, daily-process]

  - id: "MET-AH-406"
    content: |
      CBO Optimization Methodology:
      Step 1: Sort ad sets by highest CPR (cost per result)
      Step 2: Check reach â€” if < 3,000-5,000, don't kill it yet (not enough data)
      Step 3: If CPR high AND reach > 5,000 â†’ Pause
      Step 4: Sort by lowest CPR (find winners)
      Step 5: Change view to 3-7 day window â€” check if high performers have fizzled out
      Step 6: Pause fizzled ads even if lifetime numbers look good
      Step 7: For every ad killed, create a new one (duplicate, change creative/copy, publish)
      Step 8: Facebook reallocates budget automatically to remaining performers
    source: "01-Gym Launch 2.0/02-Lead Gen/04-4. How to Optimize/transcription_mp4_02-CBO_Optimization_Training.txt"

      Step 3: Increase daily budget by $5 increment
      Step 4: Check every other day â€” if still performing, increase again
      Step 5: For ads above $15 CPR â€” consider pausing and reallocating
      Step 6: View charts: check demographics (age/gender) and placements (FB vs IG)
      Step 7: Exclude non-converting demographics and placements
      Step 8: Clone best performers â†’ change targeting (different gender, age range, or location)
      Step 9: Periodically reactivate old winners with broader targeting â€” they may perform again
    source: "01-Gym Launch 2.0/02-Lead Gen/04-4. How to Optimize/transcription_mp4_03-Ad_Set_Budget_Optimization.txt"

## L4 FRAMEWORKS (FW-AH)

```yaml
- id: "FW-AH-403"
  content: |
    C-L-O-S-E-R Framework (Master Sales Framework):
    C = Clarify why they came in (What problem are we solving?)
    L = Label them with the problem (Assign it: "So your main issue is X")
    O = Overview past pain (What have you tried? Pain cycle through failures)
    S = Sell the vacation (Three-pillar pitch: Fitness, Nutrition, Accountability)
    E = Explain away concerns (Obstacle overcomes â€” handle objections)
    R = Reinforce the sale (Wow them, decrease buyer's remorse, strengthen conviction)
  source: "03-Hybrid/04-Sales/03-3. Intro To Face To Face Sales/transcription_mp4_04-C-L-O-S-E-R_Framework_Overview"
  domains: [sales, closing-framework, face-to-face, diagnostic-sale]

- id: "FW-AH-404"
  content: |
    Three-Pillar Pitch Framework:
    After exhausting pain cycle, assess each failed attempt against three pillars:
    1. FITNESS â€” Were they exercising? With a trainer? With resistance training?
    2. NUTRITION â€” Did they have a sustainable meal plan? Grocery list? Eating-out guide?
    3. ACCOUNTABILITY â€” Was anyone checking in? Weighing them? Adjusting plans?
    For each past failure, identify which pillar(s) were missing. Then show your program has all three.
    After each explanation, ask "Does that make sense?" (trial close â€” every yes increases likelihood of buying).
  source: "03-Hybrid/04-Sales/04-4. The Closing Framework/transcription_mp4_12-Hybrid_3-Pillar_Pitch + 13-Sell_The_Vacation"
  domains: [sales, pitch-technique, three-pillars, diagnostic-sale]

- id: "FW-AH-405"
  content: |
    Pain Cycle Questioning Loop:
    For each thing they've tried:
    1. "What did you do?" (They name it: Weight Watchers, gym pass, etc.)
    2. "How did that work?" (We know it didn't â€” they're here)
    3. "How long did that work?" (Stack the time/money wasted)
    Then RESTATE with amplification: "So you did 150 workouts, 225 hours of working out, and didn't lose any weight. That must be really frustrating."
    Calculate: total time cost, total emotional cost, total money spent, total different things tried.
    Key transition question: "Why do you think you've been unable to achieve this on your own?"
    Goal: They say "I need accountability" â€” then your solution is the obvious answer.
  source: "03-Hybrid/04-Sales/04-4. The Closing Framework/transcription_mp4_11-Bring_the_Pain_Question + 08-Clarify"
  domains: [sales, discovery, pain-selling, questioning-technique]

- id: "FW-AH-406"
  content: |
    Reason Close (deposit + money-back):
    "The reason we have the success rate we do, the reason 87% of people hit the goal...
    is that people make a bet. They pay $499 for the program. When you hit the goal, you get the whole thing back."
    Then immediately bridge into the epiphany story: "We tried it without a deposit â€” 150 signed up, half didn't show day 1, by end of week only 25% remained."
    Stack with satisfaction guarantee: "If I don't deliver the service you deserve, I'll give your money back myself. I've offered that for 5 years â€” only 2 people ever took me up on it."
  source: "03-Hybrid/04-Sales/04-4. The Closing Framework/transcription_mp4_14-The_Close + 15-Satisfaction_Guarantee"
  domains: [sales, closing, deposit-model, guarantee, risk-reversal]

- id: "FW-AH-407"
  content: |
    Sales Pipeline Troubleshooting Matrix:
    If leads are low: (1) Spend more until 20/day, (2) New creative (high energy), (3) Make 10 ads/week, (4) Use tested funnels, (5) Try lead ads, (6) New lead source
    If scheduling is low: (1) Check automated scheduler works, (2) Speed to contact <3 min, (3) Follow double-dial + text process
    If show rate is low: (1) Speed to contact (faster = higher show), (2) Reminder sequence (text + call), (3) Overbook 2:1 (schedule 2 per slot, expect 1 to show)
    If close rate is low: (1) Check rapport/energy/belief, (2) Spend more time on pain cycle, (3) Practice obstacle overcomes, (4) Review script
    If back-out rate is high: (1) Reinforce the sale better, (2) Don't rush end of sale, (3) Schedule both follow-up appointments
  source: "03-Hybrid/04-Sales/03-3. Intro To Face To Face Sales/transcription_mp4_07-Sales_Process_Troubleshooting_Matrix_I"
  domains: [sales, pipeline-optimization, diagnostics, troubleshooting]

- id: "FW-AH-408"
  content: |
    No-Sale Sale (Revenue Recovery Framework):
    ONLY after full pitch + deposit offer + guarantee â€” they STILL say no:
    1. Offer the same program for $0 down â€” but serviced from home (online workouts, same nutrition)
    2. Schedule their start time orientation with the paying group (they feel included)
    3. Separate no-sale customers from paying customers at orientation
    4. Sell them supplements/products at orientation (the real monetization)
    5. Some will convert to full membership after experiencing orientation
    Result: +33% more closes. Turns nos into supplementation revenue or future members.
    CRITICAL: Do not sell soft knowing this exists. Maintain full conviction on the primary sale.
  source: "03-Hybrid/04-Sales/03-3. Intro To Face To Face Sales/transcription_mp4_08-No_Sale_Sale"
  domains: [sales, revenue-recovery, no-sale-sale, downsell]

- id: "FW-AH-409"
  content: |
    Abandoned Hope Sale (Halfway-Point Resell):
    Timing: Week 3 of 6-week challenge (halfway point â€” excitement and engagement highest)
    Mindset: There are NO obstacles. Price is cheaper than current. Spouse already approved health decision. They have all info needed.
    Offer: Continue membership + credit deposit forward + get next challenge free
    Approach: ASSUMED CLOSE. This is just what everyone does as the next step.
    Target: 70-80% close rate
    Key technique: Book this meeting at the time of initial sale (during close) â€” show-up rate goes to nearly 100%
  source: "03-Hybrid/04-Sales/07-7. Resell and Ascension/transcription_mp4_02-Abandon_Hope_Overview + 01-Resell_Overview"
  domains: [sales, retention, resell, continuity, ascension]

- id: "FW-AH-410"
  content: |
    Legendary Phone Sales Script (6-part structure):
    1. THE OPEN â€” "Hey [Name]! It's [You] with [Gym], you signed up for [program] on Facebook [time ago]" â€” tonality goes UP at end of each phrase
    2. DISCOVERY â€” Age, height, weight, goal weight. "How long have you been struggling with this fat?" (have them grab their belly fat while answering)
    3. TRANSITION â€” "That makes you just like the people who best fit this program. We've helped over 1,000 people like you."
    4. PITCH â€” Three-pillar approach (Fitness + Nutrition + Accountability). "Someone is always missing something."
    5. COMMITMENTS â€” "Can you work out 3-4 days/week? Can you change food habits? Can you check in weekly?"
    6. THE CLOSE â€” Price reveal + reason close + satisfaction guarantee
  source: "03-Hybrid/04-Sales/14-14. Phone Sales/transcription_mp4_02-Legendary_Phone_Script + EFT Script PDF"
  domains: [sales, phone-sales, scripts, closing]

- id: "FW-AH-411"
  content: |
    Obstacle Overcome Classification System (4 categories of objections):
    1. STALL/DELAY: "I need to think about it" â€” Use: decision framework (3 questions), scale 1-10, same-day policy rationale, perfect program unicorn close
    2. DECISION-MAKER: "I need to talk to my spouse" â€” Use: agreement stacking, approval logic, delayed payment close
    3. MONEY: "I can't afford it" â€” Use: best/worst case, expensive clothing analogy, identity swap, gonna-spend-it-anyway, delayed payment
    4. FITNESS-SPECIFIC: "I have a bad back" / "What if I get bored" â€” Use: modification protocols, variety explanation, progression = addiction
  source: "03-Hybrid/04-Sales/05-5. Obstacle-Objection Overcomes/transcription_pdf_01-Obstacle_Overcomes"
  domains: [sales, objection-handling, overcome-framework]

- id: "FW-AH-412"
  content: |
    Pre-Sell Environment Setup (Waiting Room Framework):
    1. Greet within 10 seconds â€” run over, high energy
    2. Sign-in sheet â€” pre-filled with appointments (social proof of busy facility)
    3. Have them wait briefly (positioning â€” doctors and lawyers have waiting rooms for a reason)
    4. Hand them testimonial flipbook (laminated binder: 5-star reviews + before/afters + social media screenshots)
    5. Display community photos and event pictures
    6. Wear branded gear, clean facility, music playing
    Before you say a single word, the environment has pre-sold them.
  source: "03-Hybrid/04-Sales/04-4. The Closing Framework/transcription_mp4_02-Waiting_Room"
  domains: [sales, pre-sell, environment, social-proof, positioning]

- id: "FW-AH-413"
  content: |
    Diagnostic Sale (2-page scalable script):
    Print double-sided on clipboard. Salesperson literally reads questions.
    Page 1: Pre-meeting checklist â†’ "What changed? Why today?" â†’ Goal identification â†’ "Why is this important to you?" â†’ Identify missing pillar (fitness/nutrition/accountability) with mini-stories for each
    Page 2: "Ideal outcome 12 months from now?" â†’ Timeline estimate â†’ "Do you want to hear how the program works?" â†’ Preference questions (food type, workout time) â†’ Close with specifics
    Design principle: Make it as scalable and easy to delegate as possible. First-time salespeople close $2,400 on their first try.
  source: "02-Quick Launch/08-Diagnostic Sale/transcription_mp4_01-Diagnostic_Sale_Overview"
  domains: [sales, diagnostic-sale, scalable-sales, scripts]

- id: "FW-AH-414"
  content: |
    Green Play (Assumed Close Model):
    Two options presented as either/or:
    Option A: "Do the program on its own â€” $699, lose 25lbs, get money back"
    Option B: "Become VIP annual member â€” get the entire program at no cost"
    Assumed close: "Most people just pick their preference and we get started"
    Earn-As-You-Burn bonus: $1 off monthly membership for life per pound lost
    Result: 90%+ close rate with assumed close vs 70-80% with traditional close
    Use visual forms where they circle their choice.
  source: "04-In-GYM Launch/04-Sales/11-11. Green Play/transcription_mp4_01-Green_Play_Overview"
  domains: [sales, closing, assumed-close, green-play, offers]

- id: "FW-AH-415"
  content: |
    High Ticket Sales Script (HTP â€” $2,000-$3,000 12-week programs):
    1. BUILD RAPPORT â€” Review application, small talk, reference exceptional prior service
    2. PRE-FRAME â€” "My goal is long-term clarity. Here's where you are, what you've done, why it didn't work, what's working now. Until we find YOUR sustainable forever plan, you'll spin your wheels."
    3. THREE OPTIONS frame â€” (a) Yes, (b) No, (c) Think about it â€” but "people who don't act immediately continue to not act, so I just ask for a simple yes/no"
    4. LONG-TERM GOAL / BIG WHY â€” Dig until emotional and specific ("I don't want my kids to develop bad habits" is their SECRET WEAPON â€” circle back to this for every objection)
    5. COST OF INACTION â€” "How will [their specific pain] affect your kids/family/life if you don't fix this for good?"
    6. PRESENT SOLUTION â€” 3 options with pricing

## L5 METHODOLOGIES (MET-AH)

```yaml
- id: "MET-AH-403"
  content: |
    State Management Ritual (Pre-Sale Preparation):
    Step 1: Do your "bernie dance" or goofy physical movement â€” something that makes you laugh and smile (not a pump-up song, something PLAYFUL)
    Step 2: Read 3 testimonials of people whose lives were transformed by your program
    Step 3: Remind yourself this program genuinely changes lives
    Step 4: When prospect walks in, hit them in the face with energy â€” "Hey Susan! Happy Tuesday!"
    Step 5: Maintain this energy for every single person, whether it's the 1st or 19th that day
    Trigger: Any time you feel a cold streak coming on, repeat steps 1-3. Cold streaks = inability to control state.

- id: "MET-AH-404"
  content: |
    Pain Cycle Amplification Methodology:
    Step 1: Ask "How long have you been TRYING to get this result?" and "How long have you WANTED this?"
    Step 2: For each attempt, ask: (a) What did you do? (b) How did that work? (c) How long did it work?
    Step 3: Mentally tag each attempt â€” which pillars did they have? (F/N/A)
    Step 4: When something DID work partially, highlight what was good and note the missing pillar
    Step 5: When something failed, calculate the waste: "52 weeks Ã— 3 days = 150 workouts Ã— 1.5hrs = 225 hours and you didn't lose any weight"
    Step 6: Exhaust ALL attempts, then summarize everything
    Step 7: Ask the KEY question: "So what do you think has been holding you back?"
    Step 8: Lead them to say "I need accountability" â€” then bridge: "Totally. Can I tell you about our program?"
  source: "03-Hybrid/04-Sales/04-4. The Closing Framework/transcription_mp4_11-Bring_the_Pain_Question + 10-Overview_Past_Pain"
  domains: [sales, discovery, pain-cycle, questioning-methodology]

- id: "MET-AH-405"
  content: |
    Money Objection Stack (in order of deployment):
    1. "What's your main concern?" (isolate the real issue)
    2. Best Case / Worst Case â€” "Best: lose 26 lbs, get credit. Worst: you think we sucked, I write you a check. Real worst: walk out and stay the same"
    3. Expensive Clothing Analogy â€” "$1000 heels worn 8 times = $33/hr. Program investment: you wear your body 24/7 for life"
    4. Identity Swap â€” "We vote with our dollars. Your current identity spends on X. Your new fit identity spends on coaching."
    5. "Gonna spend it anyway" â€” "Over the next 3 months you'll spend this on dinners and shirts you'll forget"
    6. "Have you ever bought something you couldn't afford?" (yes â†’ "You made it work, right?")
    7. Delayed Payment â€” split into 2 payments aligned to paydays
    8. "If this means a lot to you, I KNOW you'll succeed" â€” the investment IS the accountability
    9. For men specifically: "Really?? I wouldn't have taken you as someone financially stressed about this" â€” ego redirect
  source: "03-Hybrid/04-Sales/05-5. Obstacle-Objection Overcomes/transcription_pdf_01-Obstacle_Overcomes"
  domains: [sales, objection-handling, money-objection, closing-techniques]

- id: "MET-AH-406"
  content: |
    "Think About It" Overcome Stack:
    1. Isolate: "What's your main concern?"
    2. 3-Question Decision Framework: (a) Do you think this will help you? (b) Do you have $X available? (c) Am I the right person? â†’ "Then we have our answers."
    3. Scale Close: "On a scale 1-10, where are you?" â†’ "What would get you from 6 to 10?" â†’ overcome that one thing
    4. Same-Day Policy rationale: "78% of our failures were people who were unsure upon signing up. We enforce same-day decisions for their good and ours."
    5. Unicorn Close: "What is this program missing that makes it not perfect? Is it the program, the price, me, or the gym?"
    6. "If this program were absolutely perfect and you KNEW you'd have your dream body, would you sign up? So what's missing?"
    7. Pre-set (during group pitch): "People who delay the decision tend to be wishywashy â€” we now ask for same-day decisions because committed people get results."
  source: "03-Hybrid/04-Sales/05-5. Obstacle-Objection Overcomes/transcription_pdf_01-Obstacle_Overcomes"
  domains: [sales, objection-handling, stall-overcome, closing-techniques]

- id: "MET-AH-407"
  content: |
    Spouse/Decision-Maker Overcome Stack:
    1. Agreement Questions: "Does your husband know you're here? Does he know you're unhappy? Does he want you healthy? Then is there any reason he'd say no?"
    2. "What if spouse says no?" â€” If "I'd do it anyway" â†’ "Let me get your ID." If "I wouldn't" â†’ "What do you think their main concern would be?" â†’ pivot to that obstacle
    3. Current Situation: "Does your spouse approve of your current situation? They DON'T. So why would they oppose you IMPROVING it?"
    4. Delayed Payment Close: "If I reserve your spot without money down, would you still want it? Great â€” let me delay your deposit to start date. If your spouse objects, I'll tear it up."
    5. Last resort: "Is this something YOU really want to do? Because my spouse would never tell me no for something I really want."
  source: "03-Hybrid/04-Sales/05-5. Obstacle-Objection Overcomes/transcription_pdf_01-Obstacle_Overcomes"
  domains: [sales, objection-handling, spouse-overcome, closing-techniques]

- id: "MET-AH-408"
  content: |
    Phone Sales Open (4-Second Hook):
    Step 1: "Hey [NAME]!" â€” use their name with confident tonality (as if they should know you)
    Step 2: Pause half a second. They say "Yeah?"
    Step 3: "It's [Your Name]" â€” tonality goes UP (as if asking "don't you know me?")
    Step 4: "with [Gym] at our [Location] location" â€” even if you only have one location (sounds bigger)
    Step 5: "You signed up for our [program] on Facebook [time frame]"
    Step 6: "I'm not going to take a bunch of your time. I have a lot of applications to get through today."

    Step 3: Write down all info from ID

```yaml
L1_PHILOSOPHIES:
  - id: "FIL-AH-403"

    domains: [self-reliance, skill-acquisition, independence]

  - id: "FIL-AH-406"
    content: "How much money you make is directly proportional to the level of skill you have. The most valuable skill above sales is generating demand - making it rain."
    source: "02-Quick Launch/01-Overview/transcription_mp4_02-1000ft_Overview_of_How_Lead_Gen_Works.txt"
    domains: [skill-value-hierarchy, lead-generation, entrepreneurship]

  - id: "FIL-AH-407"
    content: "You are not your customer. It's not about what you would opt into - it's about the numbers. Does the page convert? Does the ad convert?"

    domains: [customer-empathy, data-driven-marketing, ego-removal]

  - id: "FIL-AH-408"

    source: "02-Quick Launch/06-Creating Your Ads/transcription_mp4_01-Why_You_Should_Make_Your_Own_Ads.txt"
    domains: [pattern-interrupt, roi-focus, vanity-vs-results]

  - id: "FIL-AH-409"
    content: "The war of sales is won or lost on whether who believes more - do you believe in your product more than they believe in their excuse? That's literally all it comes down to."
    source: "GL 2.0/05-Sales/5-The Closing Environment/transcription_mp4_01-The_Unbreakable_Hot-Streak_Blueprint.txt"
    domains: [sales-belief, conviction, closing-mindset]

    domains: [client-acquisition, long-term-value, retention-mindset]

  - id: "FIL-AH-411"
    content: "No one comes in for a promotion - they come in for a prize. They don't want a 28-day transformation - they want their husband to look at them differently, their kids to see them a certain way."
    source: "GL 2.0/05-Sales/1-Sales Overview/transcription_mp4_01-Sales_Overview.txt"
    domains: [outcome-selling, emotional-selling, diagnostic-sale]

  - id: "FIL-AH-412"
    content: "It's easy to quit a gym membership, it's hard to quit a relationship. That's what we're building here - one-on-one accountability that creates relationship, not just membership."
    source: "GL 2.0/05-Sales/1-Sales Overview/transcription_mp4_01-Sales_Overview.txt"
    domains: [retention, relationship-selling, churn-reduction]

  - id: "FIL-AH-413"
    content: "We vote with our dollars about the things that we care about. If you say you value health but vote for your purse and nails, you're not aligned."
    source: "GL 2.0/05-Sales/3-Obstacles/transcription_mp4_08-Money_Closes.txt"
    domains: [sales-philosophy, value-alignment, money-objection]

  - id: "FIL-AH-414"
    content: "Don't be enchanted by top-line revenue growth. Look at your net and see what you're actually taking home each month. A $30K month at 30% margin means you only take home $10K."

    domains: [profitability, margin-focus, revenue-vs-profit]

  - id: "FIL-AH-415"

    source: "02-Quick Launch/06-Creating Your Ads/transcription_mp4_02-Lead_Gen_Scrambler.txt"
    domains: [testing-philosophy, volume-of-attempts, marketing-discipline]

  - id: "FIL-AH-416"
    content: "A consistent environment creates consistent outcomes. What we're looking for is a selling system - important for you, and critical if you want someone else to sell."
    source: "GL 2.0/05-Sales/1-Sales Overview/transcription_mp4_01-Sales_Overview.txt"
    domains: [systems-thinking, sales-environment, scalability]

  - id: "FIL-AH-417"

### L2 MENTAL_MODELS (MM-AH)

```yaml
L2_MENTAL_MODELS:
  - id: "MM-AH-403"
    content: "Skill Value Hierarchy: Training ($15/hr) â†’ Sales ($500/hr) â†’ Lead Generation (unlimited). Acquire the more valuable skill, then buy back your time at the lower rate."
    source: "02-Quick Launch/01-Overview/transcription_mp4_02-1000ft_Overview_of_How_Lead_Gen_Works.txt"
    domains: [skill-acquisition, time-leverage, value-hierarchy]

  - id: "MM-AH-404"
    content: "The Lead Gen Scrambler - systematic variation matrix: Headline x Copy (short/long) x Page Name x Image/Video x Background x Targeting = hundreds of ad combinations. Not about finding the one ad - about testing volume."
    source: "02-Quick Launch/06-Creating Your Ads/transcription_mp4_02-Lead_Gen_Scrambler.txt"
    domains: [ad-testing, creative-variation, facebook-ads]

  - id: "MM-AH-405"
    content: "Funnel Diagnostic Model: If funnel converts under 10%, the problem is your landing page, not your ads. You're getting clicks but losing them at the page. Separate ad performance from funnel performance."
    source: "02-Quick Launch/04-Setting up Leads/transcription_mp4_02-Leads_Stats_Sheet.txt"
    domains: [funnel-diagnostics, conversion-optimization, ad-performance]

  - id: "MM-AH-406"
    content: "Diagnostic Sale Model: All selling systems (phone, EFT, high-ticket, challenge, conversion) consolidated into ONE universal script. Ask questions â†’ listen â†’ diagnose â†’ prescribe. Like a doctor, not a salesperson."
    source: "GL 2.0/05-Sales/1-Sales Overview/transcription_mp4_01-Sales_Overview.txt"
    domains: [diagnostic-sale, sales-system, consultative-selling]

  - id: "MM-AH-407"
    content: "Price Anchor Cascade: Start at highest tier ($750/wk personal training) â†’ gasps â†’ step down to group+hybrid ($99/wk) â†’ step down further if needed. The gasp is necessary - if you don't get it, you didn't go high enough."
    source: "GL 2.0/05-Sales/2-Diagnostic Sale/transcription_mp4_01-Diagnostic_Sale_Overview.txt"
    domains: [pricing-psychology, price-anchoring, diagnostic-sale]

  - id: "MM-AH-408"
    content: "The Vacation Flight Analogy for pricing tiers: Private jet ($750/wk all custom 1-on-1) â†’ First class ($99/wk group+hybrid) â†’ Economy ($77/wk) â†’ Party bus ($49/wk slower results). Let them choose their flight."
    source: "GL 2.0/05-Sales/2-Diagnostic Sale/transcription_mp4_03-How_To_Close_More_Prepays.txt"
    domains: [pricing-tiers, choice-architecture, sales-framing]

  - id: "MM-AH-409"
    content: "The 1-to-10 Close Model: Ask where they are on scale of 1-10. Ask why it's not a 2 (they sell themselves on reasons). Ask what stops it from being a 10 (surface real objection). Then address only the gap between their number and 10."
    source: "GL 2.0/05-Sales/3-Obstacles/transcription_mp4_03-Stall_Close_2.txt"
    domains: [objection-handling, motivational-closing, scale-technique]

  - id: "MM-AH-410"
    content: "Banner Blindness Countermeasure: Run ads from multiple page names (gym name, personal name, 'Best Midtown Fitness'). Same ad from different identities stops people from thinking 'I've seen this before.'"
    source: "02-Quick Launch/06-Creating Your Ads/transcription_mp4_02-Lead_Gen_Scrambler.txt"
    domains: [ad-fatigue, banner-blindness, multi-identity-ads]

  - id: "MM-AH-411"
    content: "No-Sale Sale Model: When prospect says no to gym membership, offer the 42-day at-home program FREE + schedule nutrition orientation (where you sell supplements). Converts 50% close rate to 70-80% by monetizing the 'no' through a different path."
    source: "05-Resources/03-Playbook/03-42 Day Detox/transcription_mp4_02-No_Sale___Supplements_Sale.txt"
    domains: [no-sale-sale, supplement-revenue, secondary-conversion]

  - id: "MM-AH-412"
    content: "Reactivation Play as LTV Multiplier: If 10% monthly churn but 25% of cancels reactivate, effective churn = 7.5%. Run quarterly to former clients (exclude last 90 days). Four conversion avenues per play."
    source: "05-Resources/03-Playbook/07-Reactivation Play/transcription_mp4_01-Reactivation_Play_Overview.txt"
    domains: [reactivation, lifetime-value, churn-reduction]

  - id: "MM-AH-413"
    content: "Dual Calendar Model: External calendar (cold traffic ads) + Internal calendar (existing client campaigns). Internal has zero acquisition cost = pure profit. Internal plays (Big Booty, Halloween, Black Friday) add $50-60K/year net."
    source: "05-Resources/03-Playbook/12-Big Booty Bootcamp/transcription_mp4_01-Overview.txt"
    domains: [internal-marketing, revenue-stacking, marketing-calendar]

  - id: "MM-AH-414"
    content: "The Hot Streak Blueprint: Sales slumps are head game, not skill gaps. Fix by reading 3 transformational testimonials before selling. Your subconscious belief drives your delivery energy, tone, and closing power."
    source: "GL 2.0/05-Sales/5-The Closing Environment/transcription_mp4_01-The_Unbreakable_Hot-Streak_Blueprint.txt"
    domains: [sales-psychology, mindset, belief-transfer]

  - id: "MM-AH-415"
    content: "Pre-Sell Environment Stack: Greet in 10 seconds â†’ Sign-in sheet (stacked with names = scarcity) â†’ Brief wait (positioning like doctors/lawyers) â†’ Flip-book of testimonials/before-afters â†’ Then sell. They're 50% closed before you say a word."
    source: "GL 2.0/05-Sales/5-The Closing Environment/transcription_mp4_02-How_To_Get_Prospects_Reading_Credit_Cards.txt"
    domains: [closing-environment, social-proof, pre-framing]

  - id: "MM-AH-416"
    content: "Copy Power Words Hierarchy: Two most powerful words in marketing are 'free' and 'new'. Level 1 copywriting = benefits. Level 2 = tie benefits to STATUS ('fittest mom in the neighborhood'). 'So that you can' is the easiest copy transition of all time."
    source: "02-Quick Launch/06-Creating Your Ads/transcription_mp4_02-Lead_Gen_Scrambler.txt"
    domains: [copywriting, ad-copy, status-marketing]

### L3 HEURISTICS (HEUR-AH)

L3_HEURISTICS:

    domains: [ad-creative, meme-ads, click-through-rate]
    type: qualitative

  - id: "HEUR-AH-424"
    content: "Scarcity vs Urgency distinction: Scarcity = how many spots left ('if this link still works we might have a spot'). Urgency = function of time ('today is the last day' with date on page)."
    source: "02-Quick Launch/06-Creating Your Ads/transcription_mp4_02-Lead_Gen_Scrambler.txt"
    domains: [scarcity, urgency, marketing-psychology]
    type: qualitative

  - id: "HEUR-AH-425"
    content: "Always say 'fair enough?' or 'sound fair?' not 'sound great?' - you're closing on the lowest bar. As soon as they say yes to 'fair', they've closed themselves. Then: 'What card do you want to use?'"
    source: "GL 2.0/05-Sales/2-Diagnostic Sale/transcription_mp4_03-How_To_Close_More_Prepays.txt"
    domains: [closing-language, micro-commitment, fair-enough-close]
    type: qualitative

  - id: "HEUR-AH-426"
    content: "Objection handling delivery: Don't fire back instantly even if you know the answer. Pause, chew on the words, taste them. It should feel like conversation, not scripted rebuttal. Practice concepts, not exact words."
    source: "GL 2.0/05-Sales/3-Obstacles/transcription_mp4_02-Stall_Close_1.txt"
    domains: [objection-handling, delivery, conversational-selling]
    type: qualitative

  - id: "FW-AH-404"
    content: |
      Lead Gen Scrambler (Ad Variation Matrix):
      Variables to scramble:
      - HEADLINE: Duration + theme (fit mom, alpha body, get-back-in-shape, seasonal)
      - COPY: Short/long, free/new, reason-why, benefitsâ†’status, scarcity/urgency
      - PAGE NAME: Gym name, personal name, local area name (stops banner blindness)
      - IMAGE: High contrast, B&W, banners, group pics, sweaty post-class, selfies
      - VIDEO: Pitch, facility tour, whiteboard, group testimonial, average Joe testimonial, synchronized exercise, professional vs iPhone
      - MEME IT: Add captions, emojis via InShot app = higher CTR guaranteed
      - BACKGROUND: Front of facility, turf, weights area, front desk = 4X longevity
      - SUBTITLES: All videos should have subtitles
      Gender-specific ads always (male vs female targeting)
    source: "02-Quick Launch/06-Creating Your Ads/transcription_mp4_02-Lead_Gen_Scrambler.txt"
    domains: [ad-creation, creative-variation, facebook-marketing]

  - id: "FW-AH-405"
    content: |
      Diagnostic Sale Framework (The Universal Fitness Sale):
      PRE-MEETING: Pre-framed questionnaire filled, bad-habit cost noted
      STEP 1 - IDENTIFY GOAL: "What brought you in? What's your goal? What are you trying to accomplish?" (all 4 at once)
      STEP 2 - IDENTIFY GAP: "Why haven't you been successful? What's been missing?" â†’ They say: fitness, nutrition, or accountability
      STEP 3 - STORY-TELL each gap (sustainable fitness, nutrition they enjoy, accountability)
      STEP 4 - CALCULATE TIMELINE: Goal weight Ã· 2lbs/week = loss weeks + half for maintenance
      STEP 5 - PRESCRIBE (questions): Gym or home? Scheduled or convenience? Template or custom nutrition? Same food or variety? Group or 1-on-1?
      STEP 6 - RECAP their answers back to them
      STEP 7 - "Where do you want to go from here?" â†’ wait for price question
      STEP 8 - TOSS PROMOTION: "Given your goal of X lbs, we can both agree 6 weeks won't get you there"
      STEP 9 - PRICE CASCADE: Start high (guarantee results), offer PIF 20% off, half-down 10% off, minimum $299 down
      STEP 10 - STEAMROLL: "Which means when you invest this, you're going to be [goal weight] one way or another. This problem will finally be solved."
    source: "GL 2.0/05-Sales/2-Diagnostic Sale/transcription_mp4_01-Diagnostic_Sale_Overview.txt"
    domains: [sales-framework, diagnostic-sale, consultative-selling, fitness-sales]

  - id: "FW-AH-406"
    content: |
      Prepay Maximization Framework (80% Prepay Rate):
      1. Use Vacation Flight Analogy: Private Jet ($750/wk) â†’ First Class ($99/wk) â†’ Economy ($77/wk) â†’ Party Bus ($49/wk)
      2. Recommend First Class (group + hybrid) as best fit
      3. Calculate total: weeks Ã— rate
      4. Close with: "What most people do is prepay and save 20%. Sound fair?"
      5. If yes â†’ "What card do you want to use?"
      6. If hesitant â†’ "Two types of payment plans: you can choose how much you pay, or choose when you pay. What works?"
      7. Always use "sound fair?" not "sound great?" (lowest bar close)
    source: "GL 2.0/05-Sales/2-Diagnostic Sale/transcription_mp4_03-How_To_Close_More_Prepays.txt"
    domains: [pricing, prepay, payment-structure, closing]

  - id: "FW-AH-407"
    content: |
      Pre-Sell Closing Environment Stack:
      1. GREET within 10 seconds (must be inbred culture)
      2. SIGN-IN SHEET on clipboard (stacked full = social proof + scarcity)
      3. SEAT them in waiting area (brief wait = positioning, like doctors/lawyers)
      4. HAND flipbook of testimonials (5-star reviews, before/afters, FB screenshots, community events interwoven)
      5. WALL OF RESULTS visible (poster-board transformations, clothing hung on wall, custom measurements)
      6. MUSIC playing (creates energy)
      7. TRAINER introduces and begins diagnostic sale
    source: "GL 2.0/05-Sales/5-The Closing Environment/transcription_mp4_02-How_To_Get_Prospects_Reading_Credit_Cards.txt"
    domains: [closing-environment, social-proof, pre-sell, sales-setup]

  - id: "FW-AH-408"
    content: |
      Obstacle Handling System (3 Categories):
      STALL CLOSES: "Do you need 2-3 days or 2-3 months to think?" â†’ Isolate the 3 questions (will it work? have money? want to do it?) | 1-to-10 scale close | Delay payment close (last resort)
      SPOUSE CLOSES: "What if they say no?" â†’ Either "I'd get it anyway" (close) or "He won't" (close) | "Better to ask forgiveness than permission" | "Your spouse never stops you from something you REALLY want"
      MONEY CLOSES: "I don't think you have a budget issue, I think you have a priorities issue - we vote with our dollars" | "95% of people sitting where you are were over budget - they always make it work" | LA Fitness comparison close | "The cost of waiting is higher" | ACH via bank app (forgot wallet overcome)
    source: "GL 2.0/05-Sales/3-Obstacles/ (all 8 files synthesized)"
    domains: [objection-handling, sales-closes, obstacle-overcomes]

  - id: "FW-AH-409"
    content: |
      Challenge Playbook Architecture (External Plays = Cold Traffic):
      - 6-WEEK CHALLENGE: Core front-end play. Fitness + nutrition + accountability. Free or paid entry. Funnel captures leads.
      - 42-DAY DETOX: Works as both external AND internal. Supplement-focused nutrition. Membership portal included. Also powers the No-Sale Sale.
      - 28-DAY CHALLENGE: Faster conversion to EFT. Same structure as 6-week, shorter duration. Abandon hope pitch at week 3.
      - 12-WEEK HTP (High Ticket Program): $750/wk personal to $99/wk group+hybrid. Custom nutrition, custom training, Boxer access, weekly check-ins, Zoom calls. Swag bag onboarding.
      - REACTIVATION PLAY: 21-day free to former clients (90+ days ago). Quarterly max. 4 conversion avenues.
    source: "05-Resources/03-Playbook/ (sections 01-07 synthesized)"
    domains: [challenge-types, playbook, external-marketing, offer-architecture]

  - id: "FW-AH-410"
    content: |

      - LEAN FOR HALLOWEEN: 6 weeks pre-Oct 31. Candy swap nutrition overlay. $99-199. Zero fulfillment (at-home MetCon workouts). Costume party event at end.
      - BIG BOOTY BOOTCAMP: $150-200. 20% of EFT clients target. Run twice/year. Hilarious marketing emails reinforce culture.
      - 6-WEEK 6-PACK: Male-targeted version of challenge plays.
      - 28-DAY BELLY FAT BLAST: Supplement-driven. 13-day promo cycle. Story-selling origin of product.
      - ACCOUNTABILITY CHALLENGE: Holiday retention play. $49-99. Half to Toys for Tots. Christmas party. Goal = save EFTs from cancelling, not profit.
      - BRING A FRIEND: Referral-based internal play.
    source: "05-Resources/03-Playbook/ (sections 08-16 synthesized)"
    domains: [internal-plays, seasonal-marketing, retention, revenue-stacking]

  - id: "FW-AH-411"
    content: |
      Client Retention Tracking System (4 Horsemen):
      1. CLIENT COMMUNICATION: Text every 14 days min. Track last-text-out and last-text-back. 4 escalating attempts if no response â†’ then call.
      2. HANDWRITTEN CARDS: Every 3 months per client. Track dates sent.
      3. VISIT TRACKING: Check Wednesday/Thursday. Zero visits = trigger outreach. One missed week â†’ starts rolling cancellation pattern.
      4. EXIT INTERVIEWS: Conducted for every cancel. Stored in Google Drive. Track cancel reason for pattern analysis.
      + Attrition tracking: Monthly net clients = new signups - cancels. If pattern at month X, proactive outreach at month X-1.
    source: "05-Resources/03-Playbook/08-Internal Plays/transcription_mp4_01-Internal_Plays.txt"

    content: |

      Key metrics to update daily:
      - Ad Spend (from Ads Manager, sum all active campaigns)
      - Unique Clicks (add custom column in FB Ads Manager)
      Auto-calculated: Cost Per Lead, Funnel Conversion Rate
      Diagnostic: Funnel < 10% = change landing page. High CPL + high conversion = change ads.
    source: "02-Quick Launch/04-Setting up Leads/transcription_mp4_02-Leads_Stats_Sheet.txt"
    domains: [analytics, lead-tracking, kpi-dashboard, funnel-metrics]

  - id: "FW-AH-413"
    content: |
      Facebook Ad Compliance Framework (How Not to Get Banned):
      - Don't promise specific outcomes ("lose 6 pounds in 6 days" = banned)
      - Don't call out people ("are you fat?" = banned)
      - Don't promote ideal body image
      - Don't use copyrighted music in gym videos
      - Must have privacy policy on all funnel pages
      - Landing page must deliver what ad promises
      - Landing page must offer ONE thing only
      - Avoid word "free" in ad AND funnel to reduce risk
      - Don't place links in ad copy, use button only
      - No before/after photos in funnel
      - Customize funnel images (facial recognition detects duplicates across 1000 funnels)
      - Use backup ad accounts (multiple Business Manager accounts + backup payment methods)
    source: "02-Quick Launch/06-Creating Your Ads/transcription_mp4_05-Warning + 10-Facebook_Ad_Account_Tips"
    domains: [facebook-compliance, ad-policy, risk-mitigation]

    content: |

      1. PROCESS PAYMENT at point of sale (never let them walk out without processing)
      2. SCHEDULE orientation/weigh-in immediately (Saturday AM early or Monday PM)
      3. REINFORCE: Owner sends personal text welcome message ("I see you're starting Saturday at 9am")
      Max 2 people per 30-min slot. 4/hour. 3 hours = 12 per session.
    source: "GL 2.0/05-Sales/4-Post Sale/transcription_mp4_01-Post_Sale_To-Do.txt"

  source: "04-Retention/02-Attendance_Tracking"
  domains: [retention, implementation, systems]

- id: "MET-AH-087"
  content: "Exit Interview Process: 1) Schedule within 48 hours of cancellation notice, 2) In-person or phone (not text), 3) Standardized 5-question format, 4) Listen more than talk, 5) Thank them sincerely, 6) Record answers in CRM, 7) Compile themes monthly, 8) Share insights in monthly meeting"
  source: "04-Retention/05-Exit_Interviews"
  domains: [retention, data-collection, process]

- id: "MET-AH-088"
  content: "Member Events Implementation: 1) Set 12-month calendar at year start, 2) Announce each event 4-6 weeks ahead, 3) Assign one team member as event owner, 4) Set budget per event, 5) Decorate your own space (no expensive rentals), 6) Get entire staff involved, 7) Mix social + educational, 8) Post photos + tag members, 9) Survey satisfaction after"
  source: "04-Retention/04-Member_Events"
  domains: [retention, events, implementation]

- id: "MET-AH-089"
  content: "Handwritten Card System: 1) Buy 100 blank cards in bulk, 2) Schedule: birthdays (CRM reminder), enrollment anniversaries, tough weeks, random appreciation, 3) Personal message referencing specific interaction, 4) Mail (not hand-deliver) for surprise effect, 5) Train staff to also write cards"
  source: "04-Retention/03-Handwritten_Cards"
  domains: [retention, personal-touch, implementation]

- id: "MET-AH-090"
  content: "Reach-Out System Setup: 1) Choose tool (Voxer/Skipio), 2) Set automated welcome sequence (days 1, 3, 7), 3) Weekly check-in templates (personal, not canned), 4) Attendance-triggered outreach (3-day absence), 5) Monthly random appreciation, 6) Have staff record personal voice/video messages"
  source: "04-Retention/01-Reach_Outs"
  domains: [retention, automation, communication]

- id: "MET-AH-091"
  content: "Promotion Evaluation Process: 1) Identify need for the role, 2) Assess internal candidates against criteria (not just tenure), 3) Give trial project for 30 days, 4) Get peer feedback anonymously, 5) Have candidate shadow role model, 6) Written offer with new 4Rs, 7) 90-day probation with checkpoints at 30/60/90"
  source: "06-Owner Training/06-Coaching/11-Promoting_from_Within"
  domains: [promotions, evaluation, implementation]

- id: "MET-AH-092"
  content: "Core Tenets Rollout: 1) Draft 3-7 values in your authentic voice, 2) Vet with 2-3 key team members, 3) Schedule team meeting specifically for this, 4) Present each with personal story behind it, 5) Get team input and minor adjustments, 6) Print and display everywhere, 7) Reference in every hiring decision and performance review"
  source: "06-Owner Training/02-Foundation/02-Core_Tenets"
  domains: [culture, implementation, leadership]

- id: "MET-AH-093"
  content: "Financial Transparency Rollout: 1) Prepare all numbers (gross, net, expenses, reinvestment), 2) Schedule dedicated meeting, 3) Acknowledge it's uncomfortable, 4) Show full picture, 5) Explain relationship between core functions and financials, 6) Show bad month vs good month comparison, 7) Ask for questions, 8) Make it a monthly cadence"
  source: "06-Owner Training/02-Foundation/06-Sharing_Your_Numbers"
  domains: [transparency, implementation, culture]

- id: "MET-AH-094"
  content: "Compensation Structure Change Process: 1) Analyze current model vs needed model, 2) Calculate impact on each team member, 3) Prepare candid explanation ('here's what we thought, here's how we were wrong'), 4) Schedule team meeting, 5) Present new structure with rationale, 6) Provide transition period, 7) Document in writing"
  source: "08-Presentations/02-Summit-Jun-2018/17-Best_Practices_QA"
  domains: [compensation, change-management, communication]

- id: "MET-AH-095"
  content: "Price Increase Implementation: 1) Decide new price point ($150-200 target), 2) Grandfather existing members OR do town hall meeting with new terms, 3) Give 2-week trial at new service level, 4) Expect 10-30% member loss but 2-3x revenue per remaining, 5) Reinvest margin into better experience"
  source: "08-Presentations/02-Summit-Jun-2018/14-Profit_Multiplication"
  domains: [pricing, implementation, revenue]

- id: "MET-AH-096"
  content: "Emotional Intelligence Daily Practice: 1) Set aside 15 min alone time daily (no phone, no computer), 2) Name what you're feeling (specific, not 'fine'), 3) Ask 'why am I feeling this?', 4) Allow the feeling without acting on it, 5) Choose a productive thought to replace destructive one, 6) Repeat daily for 30 days minimum"
  source: "06-Owner Training/08-Leadership/08-Building_Emotional_Intelligence"
  domains: [emotional-intelligence, self-development, daily-practice]

- id: "MET-AH-097"
  content: "Delegation Transfer Process: 1) List everything on your plate, 2) Categorize: love+great at, hate+suck at, 3) Delegate hate+suck first, 4) For each delegated item: write SOP, train person, shadow them, let them do it while you watch, then let go, 5) Check in weekly for first month, then monthly"
  source: "06-Owner Training/02-Foundation/09-Delegate_to_Elevate"
  domains: [delegation, implementation, operations]

- id: "MET-AH-098"
  content: "Culture Assessment Implementation: 1) Download culture checklist, 2) Rate each item 1-5 with spouse/partner, 3) Identify top 3 deficits, 4) Prioritize by impact on business, 5) Create action plan for top deficit, 6) Reassess every 2 quarters"
  source: "06-Owner Training/02-Foundation/03-Creating_Culture"
  domains: [culture, assessment, implementation]

- id: "MET-AH-099"
  content: "CLOSER Execution Script: Step 1 'Hey, what made you come in today?' â†’ Step 2 'So you need to [label problem]? How much [specific metric]?' â†’ Step 3 'What have you tried before? How did that work out?' â†’ Step 4 'Can I tell you about our program? [Paint the result, not the process]' â†’ Step 5 '[Handle objection with specific framework]' â†’ Step 6 'You won't regret this â€” here's what happens next [onboarding]'"
  source: "08-Presentations/03-Summit-Jan-2018/06-Sales_HTP"
  domains: [sales, closing, scripting]

- id: "MET-AH-100"
  content: "Scoreboard Setup: 1) Identify 3-5 key metrics per role, 2) Get whiteboard or TV display, 3) Update daily (assign owner), 4) Review in weekly meeting, 5) Monthly champion recognition, 6) Quarterly competition with prizes, 7) Keep visible to entire team at all times"
  source: "07-Team Training/03-Scoreboard"
  domains: [accountability, implementation, gamification]

- id: "MET-AH-101"
  content: "Client Tracking Sheet Setup: 1) Create per-member sheet in CRM, 2) Include: goals, measurements, attendance log, coach notes, reach-out history, 3) Review before every interaction, 4) Update after every interaction, 5) Use for personalization in conversations, 6) Flag at-risk patterns"
  source: "04-Retention/01-Client_Tracking_Sheets"
  domains: [retention, crm, implementation]

- id: "MET-AH-102"
  content: "Consistency Habit Building (ROI on Life): 1) Pick ONE boring task you avoid, 2) Schedule it same time daily, 3) Track completion for 30 days, 4) Don't optimize â€” just do it, 5) After 30 days add second habit, 6) The boring work done consistently builds the muscle that wins"
  source: "05-ROI on Life/02-Consistency_Theory"
  domains: [consistency, habit-building, self-development]

- id: "MET-AH-103"
  content: "Weekly Marriage Superset Execution: 1) Schedule same time weekly (no work talk for first 15 min), 2) Personal check-in: how are you feeling about us? 3) Business review: what went well, what needs adjusting, 4) Alignment: are we still on the same page with vision? 5) Action items for next week"
  source: "06-Owner Training/03-Communication/12-Marriage_Superset"
  domains: [partnerships, communication, relationships]

- id: "MET-AH-104"
  content: "Churn Calculation and Capacity Planning: Step 1 - Count members who cancelled last month Ã· total members = attrition rate, Step 2 - Monthly sign-ups Ã· attrition rate = theoretical max capacity, Step 3 - To grow: either increase sign-ups OR decrease attrition, Step 4 - Managing attrition is cheaper than acquiring more leads"
  source: "10-Bonus Training/09-Calculating_Churn"
  domains: [metrics, retention, capacity-planning]

- id: "MET-AH-105"
  content: "Monthly Internal Survey Execution: 1) Use anonymous tool (WooFoo, Google Forms), 2) 5-7 questions: culture rating, manager satisfaction, workload, suggestions, NPS, 3) Distribute first of month, 4) Close by day 7, 5) Compile results, 6) Share themes in monthly meeting, 7) Act on top 2 issues"
  source: "06-Owner Training/03-Communication/13-Monthly_Surveys"
  domains: [culture, feedback, implementation]

- id: "MET-AH-106"
  content: "OOP (Out of Office Protocol) Setup: 1) List every decision you make in a typical week, 2) Categorize by urgency and complexity, 3) Assign each to a specific team member, 4) Write decision criteria for each, 5) Define 'escalate to me' threshold, 6) Test with 1-day absence, 7) Debrief what broke"
  source: "06-Owner Training/03-Communication/16-OOP"
  domains: [delegation, operations, systems]

- id: "MET-AH-107"
  content: "Bolt-On Revenue Implementation Steps: 1) Research supplement distributor (Prestige Labs or similar), 2) Set up display in facility, 3) Train staff on products (they must believe in them), 4) Bundle with membership at enrollment, 5) Track revenue per member monthly, 6) Reinvest portion into customer experience"
  source: "10-Bonus Training/01-Bolt-On_Revenue_Streams"
  domains: [revenue, implementation, operations]

- id: "MET-AH-108"
  content: "Resell Conversion Process: Plant seeds from day one ('our goal is to earn your business for life') â†’ Weekly personalized reach-outs â†’ Floor interactions by name â†’ Progress conversations about 3-month/6-month future â†’ Paint picture of upcoming events â†’ Week 3 formal transition conversation â†’ Continue selling after they convert"
  source: "08-Presentations/01-Summit-Jan-2019/09-Resell"
  domains: [retention, conversion, implementation]
